title: Epistemic Practice Conceptual Structure Node
description: A single node in the taxonomy and instance graph of epistemic practice. Each node is an epistemic entity (category, artifact, tool, agent, process, standard, etc.) with its relational context.

id.description: Unique identifier for the node; used as target in edges.

name.description: Human-readable name of the epistemic entity.

inheritanceLevel.description: Distance from the root category in the subtype hierarchy (0 = abstract top-level category).

description.description: Textual definition or characterization of the node.

roles.description: Functional role(s) the node plays in the epistemic system (e.g., 'abstract_category', 'artifact', 'inference tool', 'norm', 'sensor').

history.description: Optional genealogical record of the node's intellectual or institutional development.

specific.description: Category-specific structured properties. The exact shape depends on the node's top-level category (determined by edges of type 'subtype_of'). Common shapes are documented in the system but not enforced by the base schema.

edges.description: Directed, typed relations to other nodes in the graph.

meta.description: Provenance and quality metadata about the node's record itself.

tags.description: Free-text labels for filtering and grouping.

confidence.description: Curator's confidence in the correctness/adequacy of this node's representation.
